automation_boundaries: validate required evidence and safety boundaries
create sanitized JSON and Markdown reports
run safe validators and read-only evidence checks
leave state-changing drills as manual-run candidates
current_status: Implemented as a safe catalog/report/validator. Destructive drills, write resume, failover promote, financial correction, partner key retirement, and AI recovery adoption remain human-approved.

failure_signals: successful write while write path is unavailable
drills.goal: Confirm that unavailable PostgreSQL write path returns 503 + Retry-After and never reports financial completion.
drills.name: PostgreSQL Write Suspend / DB Down Drill
drills.safety_boundary: The drill may stop PostgreSQL locally, so PH9 records it as a manual-run candidate instead of running it by default.
drills.sensitive_data_policy: Plain financial identifiers, request payload content, signing material, authorization headers, and database URLs are prohibited.
success_criteria: new financial writes are not successful while DB writes are unavailable

drills.goal: Create out-of-band incident evidence during DB failure without depending on the database.
drills.name: Incident Artifact / Sanitized Report Drill
drills.safety_boundary: PH2 default command creates sanitized local evidence only.
drills.sensitive_data_policy: Plain financial identifiers, retry keys, request payload content, signing material, authorization headers, and database URLs are prohibited.

drills.goal: Classify incident artifacts with deterministic rules and count-only consistency summaries.
drills.name: Incident Analyzer MVP Drill
drills.safety_boundary: Analyzer output is advisory and never executes recovery.
drills.sensitive_data_policy: Only sanitized PH2 evidence and count summaries are allowed.

drills.goal: Record recovery cases and quarantine evidence while keeping execution blocked until operator approval.
drills.name: Recovery Case / Quarantine / Manual Approval Drill
drills.safety_boundary: PH4 list commands are safe; case creation needs prepared evidence.
drills.sensitive_data_policy: Recovery evidence uses masked or internal identifiers only.

drills.goal: Detect stale processing and count-only reconciliation issues without automatically completing or failing financial events.
drills.name: Stale PROCESSING Reconciliation Drill
drills.safety_boundary: PH5 proposes recovery cases and does not mutate money state.
drills.sensitive_data_policy: Reports contain counts and masked recovery references only.
success_criteria: stale records are counted after threshold
manual action candidate is generated only as evidence

drills.goal: Convert incident, recovery, and reconciliation evidence into allowlist-based AI-safe context.
drills.name: AI-safe Context Sanitizer Drill
drills.safety_boundary: PH6 creates context only; it does not call external AI APIs or execute recovery.
drills.sensitive_data_policy: Only approved masked values, tokens, hashes, counts, and statuses are allowed.
success_criteria: redaction summary contains no original unsafe value

expected_evidence: next dry-run rejection on write API
failure_signals: staged key accepted on write path
drills.goal: Verify current, previous, revoked, disabled, and next dry-run HMAC cases without exposing signing material.
drills.name: Partner HMAC Rotation Drill
drills.safety_boundary: The drill verifies rotation cases only. Final key retirement remains an approval decision.
drills.sensitive_data_policy: Evidence may include key status and hashes, but never signing material or request payload content.
success_criteria: current and bounded previous cases pass

failure_signals: HA described as replacing consistency gate
drills.goal: Explain why direct PostgreSQL transaction plus fail-closed remains current behavior and why queue-first needs ACCEPTED/COMPLETED split.
drills.name: PostgreSQL HA / Durable Queue Decision Evidence
drills.safety_boundary: PH8 creates architecture evidence only; it does not provision HA or queue middleware.
drills.sensitive_data_policy: Architecture evidence contains no runtime data.
success_criteria: HA still requires consistency gate and write resume approval

follow_up_candidates.name: Latency attribution instrumentation
follow_up_candidates.note: PH9 does not implement latency attribution. k6 symptoms must be correlated with Nginx, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Redis, and external dependency metrics in a later phase.

follow_up_candidates.name: Latency drill test plan execution
follow_up_candidates.note: Latency drill targets remain candidate commands, not PH9 completed drill commands.

manual_approval_boundaries: write resume after DB recovery or failover
phase: PH9 Production Hardening Drill Plan & Evidence Runner
scope: validation of automation and manual approval boundaries
PH10/PH11 latency work linked only as follow-up candidates
